scenarios.user_content: Check my disk health and also look for any USB errors.
Run full system diagnostics - check network, disk, and USB.
I'm seeing slowdowns. Check which processes are consuming the most CPU and then look at disk health.
Something is wrong with my system. Pull device errors and also check the system event log.
Check network connectivity and then look at system events for any related errors.
Restart WSL and then run a WSL network diagnosis to confirm it recovered.
Get WSL health status and also check Docker to see if both are up.
Restart the vLLM service and then check WSL health to confirm the stack is healthy.
Stop the PC_AI-HVSockProxy service and then restart it to clear any stuck state.
Check top memory-consuming processes and then look at system events for out-of-memory warnings.
Scan for USB errors and also check device manager for any device errors.
Check disk status and pull recent system events to correlate any storage-related warnings.
I want a full picture of my hardware health: disk, network, and any device errors.
Check which processes are hogging resources and also run a network status check.
Get current Docker status and then run a WSL network tool diagnosis.
Search the system logs for disk errors and then check disk health directly.
Look up device error documentation and then pull device errors from the system.
Get system info about the OS build and then check recent system events for crashes.
Check USB status and then scan logs for any xHCI controller errors.
Run a WSL network diagnosis and then check Docker to confirm the inference stack is healthy.
Optimize the model host GPU usage and then check WSL health to confirm the change took effect.
Set the LLM provider order and then restart WSL so the new settings load.
Check for device errors and USB issues at the same time to speed up diagnosis.
Pull disk status and top processes to see if heavy I/O is causing slowdowns.
Check network status and then run the WSL network tool to compare host-level vs WSL-level connectivity.
Get system info about the GPU driver version and then check top processes to see GPU-bound tasks.
Restart WSL, get WSL health, and then check Docker status to confirm everything is up.
Check disk status, pull system events, and look at device errors for a thorough storage audit.
Check network status, USB status, and top processes to get a broad system health overview.
Stop the service, optimize the model host, and then start the service again for a clean restart.
